etymology.description: 妹(妹) (mèi, "younger sister") is the core reference here, directly pointing to the familial relationship of a female sibling younger than oneself. The repetition in 妹妹 (mèimei) often emphasizes familiarity and affection in spoken language.
etymology.interpretation: 妹(妹) (mèi, "younger sister") inherently implies the concept of a female sibling who is younger, which naturally extends to the word 妹妹 (mèimei), strengthening the idea of familial love and intimacy associated with the younger female sibling. The repetition conveys an affectionate or familiar tone similar to 'sissy' or 'sis' in English.

examples.chinese: 我的妹妹很聪明(我的妹妹很聰明)。
examples.english: My younger sister is very smart.

examples.chinese: 妹妹正在房间里玩玩具(妹妹正在房間裡玩玩具)。
examples.pinyin: Mèimei zhèngzài fángjiān lǐ wán wánjù.
examples.english: The young girl is playing with toys in the room.

examples.chinese: 我每天和妹妹一起走路上学(我每天和妹妹一起走路上學)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān hé mèimei yīqǐ zǒulù shàngxué.
examples.english: I walk to school with my younger sister every day.
